top_triggered_provisions.provision: Article V speech and press protections
Article XVII foreign policy and national security

scenarios.title: Budget Deadlock — Automatic Continuing Resolution

violations.summary: Executive branch attempts to alter funding ratios, withhold appropriated funds, or selectively apply funding levels under the automatic continuing resolution.

satisfied_duties.duty: review the executive branch's selective CR funding manipulation on an expedited basis
satisfied_duties.outcome: issued an expedited order blocking the executive branch's selective application of the automatic continuing resolution

ai_summary.primary_violation: Executive branch attempts to alter funding ratios, withhold appropriated funds, or selectively apply funding levels under the automatic continuing resolution.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Dominant Parties Impose Cartelized Ballot-Access Barriers

violations.actor: State election authorities and dominant parties
violations.source: Article I Section 9.1 and Section 9.4; Article V Section 2.5
violations.summary: State election authorities, under pressure from the dominant parties, impose extraordinary signature and filing barriers targeted at new parties and independent entrants in a manner designed to keep new parties and independent entrants off the ballot. Article I Section 9.1 creates a constitutional qualification floor for party ballot access, and Section 9.4 bars prohibiting party formation or operation on ideological or political grounds.

satisfied_duties.duty: void ballot-access barriers that functionally defeat the constitutional party-qualification rules or discriminate against new entrants for partisan advantage
satisfied_duties.outcome: voided the cartelized ballot-access barriers and ordered ballot qualification under the constitutional party-qualification rules
satisfied_duties.source: Article I Section 9.1 and Section 9.4; Article V Section 2.5

ai_summary.primary_violation: State election authorities, under pressure from the dominant parties, impose extraordinary signature and filing barriers targeted at new parties and independent entrants in a manner designed to keep new parties and independent entrants off the ballot. Article I Section 9.1 creates a constitutional qualification floor for party ballot access, and Section 9.4 bars prohibiting party formation or operation on ideological or political grounds.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Child Welfare Proceeding Without Independent Representation

violations.summary: A state court conducts a termination of parental rights proceeding affecting a child's fundamental interests without providing independent representation for the child and without stating reasons on the record for departing from the child's expressed preference, in violation of Article XVIII Section 4.5.

satisfied_duties.duty: vacate or remand the proceeding for constitutionally sufficient process including independent representation and, where the court departs materially from the child's preference, specific stated reasons grounded in the child's welfare
satisfied_duties.outcome: vacated the proceeding and remanded for constitutionally sufficient process with independent representation for the child and an on-record explanation if the court departs from the child's expressed preference

ai_summary.primary_violation: A state court conducts a termination of parental rights proceeding affecting a child's fundamental interests without providing independent representation for the child and without stating reasons on the record for departing from the child's expressed preference, in violation of Article XVIII Section 4.5.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Climate Backsliding Without Constitutionally Sufficient Replacement

violations.summary: Government carries out administrative rollback of major greenhouse-gas regulations without replacement measures without simultaneously replacing those protections with measures constitutionally sufficient in aggregate. Article XVIII Section 1.2 prohibits knowingly and materially dismantling existing climate protections without constitutionally sufficient replacement.

satisfied_duties.duty: declare the climate-protection backsliding a constitutional violation and require the government to submit a constitutionally sufficient remedial plan within a defined period
satisfied_duties.outcome: declared a constitutional violation and ordered the government to submit a constitutionally sufficient remedial climate plan within 180 days; retained jurisdiction to review the plan's adequacy

satisfied_duties.duty: adopt and submit a constitutionally sufficient remedial climate plan within the court's deadline
satisfied_duties.outcome: adopted and submitted a remedial climate plan that the court found constitutionally sufficient in aggregate

ai_summary.primary_violation: Government carries out administrative rollback of major greenhouse-gas regulations without replacement measures without simultaneously replacing those protections with measures constitutionally sufficient in aggregate. Article XVIII Section 1.2 prohibits knowingly and materially dismantling existing climate protections without constitutionally sufficient replacement.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Executive Officer Defies Congressional Subpoena

violations.summary: Secretary of Defense willfully refuses to comply with a valid congressional subpoena.

bottlenecks.summary: Secretary of Defense failed to comply with the congressional subpoena by testifying and producing required documents by day 30 under Article II Section 16.2 and Section 16.3.

satisfied_duties.duty: refer the contempt citation to the Accountability Commission for criminal prosecution
satisfied_duties.outcome: referred the contempt citation to the Accountability Commission for criminal prosecution

satisfied_duties.duty: prosecute criminal contempt within 90 days of referral or publish specific written findings for declining
satisfied_duties.outcome: initiated criminal contempt prosecution within the required 90-day window

failed_duties.duty: comply with the congressional subpoena by testifying and producing required documents
failed_duties.outcome: Secretary of Defense failed to comply with the congressional subpoena by testifying and producing required documents by day 30 under Article II Section 16.2 and Section 16.3.

ai_summary.primary_violation: Secretary of Defense willfully refuses to comply with a valid congressional subpoena.

scenarios.title: Constitutional Organs Not Constituted on Time

bottlenecks.summary: Congress and appointing authorities failed to constitute the Constitutional Organs on the timeline required by the Constitution by day 365 under Article XIX Section 5.2.
Congress and appointing authorities failed to complete the constitutional-organ appointment process after Supreme Court order by day 420 under Article XIX Section 5.2A.

satisfied_duties.duty: provide by law for the first federal elections and orderly commencement of terms under the new constitutional order

satisfied_duties.duty: order completion of the constitutional-organ appointment process
satisfied_duties.outcome: ordered completion of the constitutional-organ appointment process within 30 days

failed_duties.duty: constitute the Constitutional Organs on the timeline required by the Constitution
failed_duties.outcome: Congress and appointing authorities failed to constitute the Constitutional Organs on the timeline required by the Constitution by day 365 under Article XIX Section 5.2.

failed_duties.duty: complete the constitutional-organ appointment process after Supreme Court order
failed_duties.outcome: Congress and appointing authorities failed to complete the constitutional-organ appointment process after Supreme Court order by day 420 under Article XIX Section 5.2A.

failed_duties.duty: resolve the contested incapacity determination by 2/3 vote of both chambers
failed_duties.outcome: Congress failed to resolve the contested incapacity determination by 2/3 vote of both chambers by day 23 under Article III Section 11.3.

scenarios.title: Federal Contractor with Undisclosed Foreign Beneficial Owner

violations.summary: A major federal contractor concealed foreign-government beneficial ownership while seeking sensitive federal work. Article VIII Section 1.13 now makes that concealment itself constitutionally disqualifying even where no individual federal official took a bribe or committed a separate corruption offense.

satisfied_duties.duty: investigate whether the contractor concealed beneficial ownership in violation of Article VIII Section 1.13 and determine whether any official also engaged in regulatory corruption under Article XIII Section 9.3
satisfied_duties.outcome: completed the investigation, confirmed concealed foreign-government beneficial ownership, found no corrupt official, and referred the contract for invalidation based on Article VIII Section 1.13's disclosure requirement
satisfied_duties.source: Article VIII Section 1.13 and Article XIII Section 9.3

ai_summary.primary_violation: A major federal contractor concealed foreign-government beneficial ownership while seeking sensitive federal work. Article VIII Section 1.13 now makes that concealment itself constitutionally disqualifying even where no individual federal official took a bribe or committed a separate corruption offense.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Coordinated Absenteeism to Defeat a Constitutionally Required Vote

violations.summary: President continues military operations in violation of a withdrawal order.
House members coordinate absence and refuse available continuity procedures in order to defeat a constitutionally required vote, constituting obstruction of a constitutional process.

bottlenecks.summary: Congress failed to grant authorization for continued military force or refuse it by day 30 under Article XVI Section 1.

bottlenecks.source: Article XVI Section 1.5(e) and Article III Section 10.2A
bottlenecks.summary: House of Representatives failed to hold a recorded impeachment vote on the unlawful continued military operation by day 61 under Article XVI Section 1.5(e) and Article III Section 10.2A.

satisfied_duties.duty: issue the required withdrawal order for unauthorized continued operations
satisfied_duties.outcome: issued a withdrawal order

satisfied_duties.duty: issue a self-executing fiscal hold on appropriated funds for the unlawful operation
satisfied_duties.outcome: issued a self-executing fiscal hold on appropriated funds for the unlawful operation

satisfied_duties.duty: implement the ACC fiscal hold within 24 hours
satisfied_duties.outcome: implemented the ACC fiscal hold, cutting off appropriated funds for the unlawful operation

satisfied_duties.duty: proceed to impeachment trial on the automatically transmitted war-powers articles
satisfied_duties.outcome: began trial on automatically transmitted war-powers articles

failed_duties.duty: grant authorization for continued military force or refuse it
failed_duties.outcome: Congress failed to grant authorization for continued military force or refuse it by day 30 under Article XVI Section 1.

failed_duties.duty: hold a recorded impeachment vote on the unlawful continued military operation
failed_duties.outcome: House of Representatives failed to hold a recorded impeachment vote on the unlawful continued military operation by day 61 under Article XVI Section 1.5(e) and Article III Section 10.2A.
failed_duties.source: Article XVI Section 1.5(e) and Article III Section 10.2A

ai_summary.primary_violation: President continues military operations in violation of a withdrawal order.
ai_summary.primary_bottleneck: Congress failed to grant authorization for continued military force or refuse it by day 30 under Article XVI Section 1.

failed_duties.duty: comply with the federal court enforcement order

satisfied_duties.duty: review the warrant request for the lethal operation against the citizen
satisfied_duties.outcome: denied the warrant because capture was feasible and the constitutional standard for lethal action was not met

satisfied_duties.source: Article XIII Section 12 and Article XVI

scenarios.title: Government Purchases Data to Circumvent Warrant Requirement

violations.summary: Government agency purchases personal data from a private data broker — including location data, communications metadata, and association records — in order to obtain information it would need a warrant to collect directly, in violation of Article V Section 10.3.

satisfied_duties.duty: apply the warrant requirement to the purchased data under Article V Section 10.3 and suppress any evidence derived from it
satisfied_duties.outcome: treated the purchased data as government-obtained under Article V Section 10.3 and suppressed it as collected without a warrant

satisfied_duties.outcome: held that the domestic deployment lacked a constitutional basis and must cease

satisfied_duties.duty: require the state to adopt a lawful remedy within a defined period sufficient to restore the constitutional education floor for the affected students
satisfied_duties.outcome: required the state to adopt a constitutionally sufficient remedy for the education floor violation within one year and retained jurisdiction to review adequacy

scenarios.title: Executive Coercion of Platforms to Suppress Lawful Political Dissent

violations.source: Article V Section 2.1 and Section 2.6; Article XIII Section 2.0.5
violations.summary: Senior executive officials pressure major digital platforms through threatened regulatory retaliation to suppress lawful political dissent critical of the administration without issuing a formal order. The Constitution does not permit the executive to accomplish through coercive private pressure what Article V forbids it to do directly, and Article XIII Section 2.0.5 bars use of agency power for political retaliation.

satisfied_duties.duty: determine on expedited review whether the state-coerced suppression is attributable to government action and enjoin any unconstitutional pressure
satisfied_duties.outcome: held that the pressure campaign constituted state action through coercive use of threatened agency power, enjoined further retaliation, and ordered restoration of access where the suppression resulted from that coercion
satisfied_duties.source: Article V Section 2.1 and Section 2.6; Article XIII Section 2.0.5

satisfied_duties.duty: investigate whether executive officials used agency threats or regulatory leverage to induce viewpoint-based suppression
satisfied_duties.outcome: found that executive officials used threatened regulatory retaliation to induce viewpoint-based suppression and referred the responsible officials for prosecution or removal proceedings
satisfied_duties.source: Article XIII Section 2.0.5 and Article XII

ai_summary.primary_violation: Senior executive officials pressure major digital platforms through threatened regulatory retaliation to suppress lawful political dissent critical of the administration without issuing a formal order. The Constitution does not permit the executive to accomplish through coercive private pressure what Article V forbids it to do directly, and Article XIII Section 2.0.5 bars use of agency power for political retaliation.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

satisfied_duties.outcome: disapproved the unauthorized executive order and prevented it from taking effect

scenarios.title: Foreign Cyber Election Crisis
triggered_provisions: Article V speech and press protections
Article XVII foreign policy and national security

violations.summary: President attempts direct federal control over state election administration outside the constitutional election framework.
Executive branch attempts to suppress suspected foreign propaganda directly rather than using disclosure and transparency tools.

satisfied_duties.duty: protect the election through constitutional disclosure, transparency, and emergency administration powers
satisfied_duties.outcome: used constitutional disclosure and emergency administration powers to protect election administration without direct federal takeover

satisfied_duties.duty: review any direct speech restriction imposed in response to the foreign attack

scenarios.title: Foreign Influence Operation — Shell Entity Political Messaging

violations.source: Article VI Section 5.1(d), Section 5.3, and Section 5.5
violations.summary: Patriot Voice LLC, covertly funded and directed by a foreign government intelligence service, runs mass political messaging designed to destabilize public confidence in democratic institutions. Article VI Section 5.5 now pierces domestic conduits used to route foreign political funding, direction, or sponsorship and extends disclosure beyond formal campaign spending to mass political communications directed at the domestic public on matters of governance, elections, or constitutional legitimacy.

satisfied_duties.duty: investigate whether the shell entity is acting as a conduit for prohibited foreign political funding or direction, order disclosure of the ultimate beneficial sponsor, and determine whether the messaging violates Article VI Section 5.1(d), Section 5.3, or Section 5.5
satisfied_duties.outcome: investigated the shell entity, found it to be acting as a conduit for foreign political sponsorship under Article VI Section 5.5, and ordered public disclosure of the ultimate foreign sponsor
satisfied_duties.source: Article VI Section 5.1, Section 5.3, and Section 5.5

satisfied_duties.duty: impose civil and criminal penalties on the foreign sponsor and the shell entity operators under Article VI Section 5.1, Section 5.3, and Section 5.5
satisfied_duties.outcome: imposed civil penalties and referred the foreign sponsor's domestic operators for criminal prosecution under Article VI Section 5.1 and Section 5.3
satisfied_duties.source: Article VI Section 5.1, Section 5.3, and Section 5.5

ai_summary.primary_violation: Patriot Voice LLC, covertly funded and directed by a foreign government intelligence service, runs mass political messaging designed to destabilize public confidence in democratic institutions. Article VI Section 5.5 now pierces domestic conduits used to route foreign political funding, direction, or sponsorship and extends disclosure beyond formal campaign spending to mass political communications directed at the domestic public on matters of governance, elections, or constitutional legitimacy.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Justice Refuses Valid Recusal Demand

violations.summary: Associate Justice refuses to recuse despite a valid Judicial Conduct Board direction under Article IV Section 7.3. The refusal triggers an automatic referral and the justice is barred from the case.

satisfied_duties.duty: rule on the recusal petition and direct the justice to recuse if the petition meets the standard
satisfied_duties.outcome: directed recusal; the justice refused, triggering automatic referral and case bar under Article IV Section 7.3

satisfied_duties.duty: process the automatic recusal-refusal referral and determine whether removal proceedings are warranted
satisfied_duties.outcome: processed the recusal-refusal referral and referred the matter for impeachment proceedings as conduct-based grounds for removal

ai_summary.primary_violation: Associate Justice refuses to recuse despite a valid Judicial Conduct Board direction under Article IV Section 7.3. The refusal triggers an automatic referral and the justice is barred from the case.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Member of Congress Takes Bribes and Chamber Leadership Shields Them

violations.source: Article II Section 14.2 and Article VIII Section 1.4
violations.summary: Senior member of Congress accepts money and future consulting promises from a regulated industry in exchange for official action, constituting bribery and abuse of office.

satisfied_duties.duty: investigate and prosecute the congressional bribery matter notwithstanding any chamber preference for internal handling
satisfied_duties.outcome: opened a corruption case against the member of Congress despite chamber leadership's refusal to move on expulsion
satisfied_duties.source: Article II Section 14.2 and Article VIII Section 5.1

satisfied_duties.actor: Federal courts and relevant chamber officers
satisfied_duties.duty: give effect to automatic removal upon final bribery conviction
satisfied_duties.outcome: gave effect to automatic removal of the member from office upon bribery conviction; no chamber vote was required
satisfied_duties.source: Article II Section 5.5(a) and Article VIII Section 1.7

ai_summary.primary_violation: Senior member of Congress accepts money and future consulting promises from a regulated industry in exchange for official action, constituting bribery and abuse of office.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Naturalized Citizen Excluded From Federal Office

violations.source: Article IX Section 1 and Article III Section 2
violations.summary: A federal statute bars naturalized citizens from holding a non-presidential federal office, creating a blanket civic disability based on the manner of citizenship.

satisfied_duties.duty: void the blanket exclusion of naturalized citizens from federal office
satisfied_duties.outcome: voided the blanket exclusion of naturalized citizens from non-presidential federal office
satisfied_duties.source: Article IX Section 1 and Article III Section 2

ai_summary.primary_violation: A federal statute bars naturalized citizens from holding a non-presidential federal office, creating a blanket civic disability based on the manner of citizenship.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Nuclear First-Use Order — Secretary Refusal and Circumvention Attempt

violations.summary: President removes the Secretary of Defense to install a replacement willing to confirm a nuclear first-use order, using the removal power to circumvent a constitutional authorization safeguard.

satisfied_duties.duty: refuse to validate the unconstitutional nuclear first-use order notwithstanding the replacement pressure
satisfied_duties.outcome: also refused to validate the unconstitutional nuclear first-use order

satisfied_duties.duty: refuse to confirm an unconstitutional nuclear first-use order
satisfied_duties.outcome: refused to confirm the unconstitutional nuclear first-use order

satisfied_duties.duty: refuse to countersign or support an unconstitutional nuclear first-use order
satisfied_duties.outcome: refused to support the unconstitutional nuclear first-use order

ai_summary.primary_violation: President removes the Secretary of Defense to install a replacement willing to confirm a nuclear first-use order, using the removal power to circumvent a constitutional authorization safeguard.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Overbroad Nationwide Rights Suspension Attempt

violations.summary: Congress attempted a nationwide rights suspension that is not narrowly tailored to a geographic area or population directly affected by the emergency, in violation of Article V Section 1.3 and Section 14.3. Without the required judicial finding, the suspension does not take effect.

satisfied_duties.duty: determine whether the proposed nationwide rights suspension is narrowly tailored, necessary, and supported by a judicial finding that the emergency itself affects the entire nation as required by Article V Section 14.3
satisfied_duties.outcome: declined to issue the required judicial finding because the proposed suspension is not narrowly tailored to the affected area and population

ai_summary.primary_violation: Congress attempted a nationwide rights suspension that is not narrowly tailored to a geographic area or population directly affected by the emergency, in violation of Article V Section 1.3 and Section 14.3. Without the required judicial finding, the suspension does not take effect.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Overseas Citizen Denied Federal Electoral Home

violations.source: Article IX Section 3 and Article I
violations.summary: Election officials deny an overseas citizen any practical federal electoral home and reject registration on the theory that long residence abroad extinguished meaningful federal participation.

satisfied_duties.duty: restore a lawful federal electoral home for the overseas citizen
satisfied_duties.outcome: restored the overseas citizen's federal electoral assignment and registration rights
satisfied_duties.source: Article IX Section 3 and Article I

ai_summary.primary_violation: Election officials deny an overseas citizen any practical federal electoral home and reject registration on the theory that long residence abroad extinguished meaningful federal participation.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Senior Official Commits Perjury Before Congress

violations.summary: Secretary of State gives testimony before a congressional committee containing knowing false statements of material fact, constituting perjury before Congress under Article II Section 16.4.

bottlenecks.summary: Accountability Commission failed to indict the witness for congressional perjury if supported by the evidence by day 35 under Article II Section 16.4(b).

satisfied_duties.duty: refer the perjury matter to the Accountability Commission
satisfied_duties.outcome: referred the congressional perjury matter to the Accountability Commission for exclusive prosecution

failed_duties.duty: indict the witness for congressional perjury if supported by the evidence
failed_duties.outcome: Accountability Commission failed to indict the witness for congressional perjury if supported by the evidence by day 35 under Article II Section 16.4(b).

scenarios.title: Major Platform Denies Equal Candidate Access to Political Distribution Tools

violations.summary: a major digital platform denies a lawfully registered federal candidate equal access to political advertising and distribution tools available to competing candidates. Article VI Section 7.5(c) requires major digital platforms to provide equal access to such tools for all lawfully registered political candidates and parties.

satisfied_duties.duty: review the unequal platform access, order immediate nondiscriminatory access if a violation is found, and require an independent audit of the platform's political-content distribution tools
satisfied_duties.outcome: found a violation of Article VI Section 7.5(c), ordered immediate equal access to the platform's political advertising and distribution tools, and opened an independent audit of the platform's political-content systems
satisfied_duties.source: Article VI Section 7.5 and Article XII

ai_summary.primary_violation: a major digital platform denies a lawfully registered federal candidate equal access to political advertising and distribution tools available to competing candidates. Article VI Section 7.5(c) requires major digital platforms to provide equal access to such tools for all lawfully registered political candidates and parties.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Dominant Platform Manipulates Emergency and Election Information Visibility

violations.source: Article VI Section 7.5 and Section 7.5A; Article I
violations.summary: a dominant digital platform materially downranks or obscures lawful emergency and election-administration information during a live constitutional emergency or election-administration disruption. Article VI Section 7.5A permits temporary corrective relief where manipulative visibility practices by a dominant platform materially impair access to official emergency or election-administration information.

satisfied_duties.duty: review the manipulation claim, compel disclosure of relevant ranking criteria, and order temporary neutral restoration of access to official emergency or election-administration information if Article VI Section 7.5A is triggered
satisfied_duties.outcome: compelled disclosure of ranking criteria and audit information, found manipulative suppression of official emergency or election-administration information, and ordered temporary neutral restoration of visibility under Article VI Section 7.5A
satisfied_duties.source: Article VI Section 7.5 and Section 7.5A; Article XII

satisfied_duties.duty: review the temporary visibility-restoration order on an expedited basis and sustain it if it is narrowly limited to preserving access to official emergency or election-administration information
satisfied_duties.outcome: upheld the temporary restoration order as a narrow election-and-emergency continuity measure rather than a general power to control private editorial rankings

ai_summary.primary_violation: a dominant digital platform materially downranks or obscures lawful emergency and election-administration information during a live constitutional emergency or election-administration disruption. Article VI Section 7.5A permits temporary corrective relief where manipulative visibility practices by a dominant platform materially impair access to official emergency or election-administration information.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

bottlenecks.summary: House of Representatives failed to hold the required impeachment vote on the PMC substitution violation by day 26 under Article III Section 10.2A and Article XVI Section 4.4.

satisfied_duties.duty: proceed to impeachment trial on the automatically transmitted PMC-substitution articles
satisfied_duties.outcome: began impeachment trial on the automatically transmitted PMC substitution articles

failed_duties.duty: hold the required impeachment vote on the PMC substitution violation
failed_duties.outcome: House of Representatives failed to hold the required impeachment vote on the PMC substitution violation by day 26 under Article III Section 10.2A and Article XVI Section 4.4.
failed_duties.source: Article III Section 10.2A and Article XVI Section 4.4

ai_summary.primary_violation: President deploys private military contractors in a combat role to conduct the operation Congress denied, in direct violation of Article XVI Section 4.4, which prohibits using PMCs to avoid authorization requirements or to conceal the scale of US military engagement.
ai_summary.primary_bottleneck: House of Representatives failed to hold the required impeachment vote on the PMC substitution violation by day 26 under Article III Section 10.2A and Article XVI Section 4.4.

satisfied_duties.outcome: voided the restriction on political speech on expedited review

satisfied_duties.duty: reject the residual immunity claim asserted by the former President

violations.summary: President interferes with an investigation into their own conduct.

scenarios.title: Coordinated Private Infrastructure Cutoff of Lawful Opposition Organization

violations.source: Article VI Section 7.5A; Article XII; Article V Section 2.6
violations.summary: dominant payment processors and hosting firms jointly cut off service to a lawful opposition organization operating nationally during a federal election period. Article VI Section 7.5A permits neutral continuity and nondiscrimination duties for dominant civic intermediaries where coordinated denial would materially impair lawful political competition.

satisfied_duties.duty: determine whether the excluded organization is a lawful qualified political actor and order temporary nondiscriminatory restoration of essential service if Article VI Section 7.5A is triggered
satisfied_duties.outcome: found that the coordinated cutoff threatened meaningful federal political competition, ordered temporary restoration of nondiscriminatory payment and hosting access under Article VI Section 7.5A, and directed expedited judicial review of the order

satisfied_duties.duty: review the temporary restoration order on an expedited basis and sustain it if the targeted firms are dominant civic intermediaries whose coordinated denial materially impairs lawful federal political competition
satisfied_duties.outcome: upheld the temporary restoration order as a narrowly tailored, viewpoint-neutral continuity measure against coordinated exclusion by dominant civic intermediaries during a federal election period
satisfied_duties.source: Article VI Section 7.5A; Article V Section 2.6

ai_summary.primary_violation: dominant payment processors and hosting firms jointly cut off service to a lawful opposition organization operating nationally during a federal election period. Article VI Section 7.5A permits neutral continuity and nondiscrimination duties for dominant civic intermediaries where coordinated denial would materially impair lawful political competition.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Deliberate Destruction of Federal Records During Transition

violations.source: Article XIX Section 6.5 and Article VI Section 1.1
violations.summary: Outgoing administration officials destroyed or concealed non-electoral federal records during transition. Article XIX Section 6.5 makes deliberate destruction or concealment of public records during transition anti-subversion conduct and independently preserves all federal records beyond the election-records-specific protection in Article VI Section 1.2(c).

satisfied_duties.duty: investigate the records destruction, determine responsibility under Article XIX Section 6.5 and Article VI Section 1.1, and prosecute responsible officials
satisfied_duties.outcome: investigated, determined that the records destruction violated Article XIX Section 6.5 as anti-subversion conduct, and filed charges under Article VI Section 1.6
satisfied_duties.source: Article XIX Section 6.5 and Article VI Section 1.6

satisfied_duties.duty: review the pre-enactment single-subject challenge
satisfied_duties.outcome: voided the omnibus bill and returned the matter for single-subject redrafting

scenarios.title: Dominant Parties Coordinate Soft Cartelization Without Formal Ballot Exclusion

violations.source: Article I Section 9.5 and Section 10.6; Article V Section 2.5
violations.summary: Dominant parties and affiliated intermediaries coordinate to deny qualified new entrants access to debates, data feeds, and routine election-information channels while preserving formal ballot access. Article I Section 9.5 now protects nondiscriminatory access to core public or publicly regulated election infrastructure necessary for meaningful federal contestation.

satisfied_duties.duty: determine whether the excluded entrants are lawfully qualified and order temporary nondiscriminatory access to core public or publicly regulated election infrastructure under Article I Section 9.5 and Section 10.6
satisfied_duties.outcome: found that qualified entrants were being discriminatorily excluded from core public or publicly regulated election infrastructure, ordered temporary nondiscriminatory access, and required neutral participation criteria for the affected channels
satisfied_duties.source: Article I Section 9.5 and Section 10.6; Article V Section 2.5

satisfied_duties.duty: review the temporary access order on an expedited basis and sustain it if it is limited to core public or publicly regulated election infrastructure necessary for meaningful federal contestation
satisfied_duties.outcome: upheld the temporary access order as a narrow remedy preserving meaningful electoral competition without compelling private ideological sponsorship beyond the constitutional floor
satisfied_duties.source: Article I Section 9.5 and Section 10.6; Article V Section 2.5

ai_summary.primary_violation: Dominant parties and affiliated intermediaries coordinate to deny qualified new entrants access to debates, data feeds, and routine election-information channels while preserving formal ballot access. Article I Section 9.5 now protects nondiscriminatory access to core public or publicly regulated election infrastructure necessary for meaningful federal contestation.

satisfied_duties.outcome: applied strict scrutiny and voided the discriminatory state classification for failure to serve a compelling interest by the least restrictive means

satisfied_duties.duty: review the pattern of judicial delay and determine whether it reflects strategic misconduct rather than genuine complexity or workload

failed_duties.duty: issue a written decision in the constitutionally expedited case

violations.summary: The Electoral Stability Amendment attempts to evade the unamendable core or principled backstop by structurally entrenching political power.

satisfied_duties.duty: issue a pre-clearance ruling on the proposed amendment
satisfied_duties.outcome: rejected the proposed amendment at pre-clearance and published a written opinion stating that it would materially impair the capacity of citizens to alter their government through free and regular elections and would functionally evade the unamendable protections of universal suffrage and binding election results
satisfied_duties.source: Article XI Section 4.1, Section 4.3, and Section 4.4

scenarios.title: Unauthorized Sustained Military Action

violations.summary: President continues military operations in violation of a withdrawal order.

bottlenecks.summary: Congress failed to grant authorization for continued military force or refuse it by day 30 under Article XVI Section 1.

bottlenecks.source: Article XVI Section 1.5(e) and Article III Section 10.2A
bottlenecks.summary: House of Representatives failed to hold a recorded impeachment vote on the unlawful continued military operation by day 61 under Article XVI Section 1.5(e) and Article III Section 10.2A.

satisfied_duties.duty: issue the required withdrawal order for unauthorized continued operations
satisfied_duties.outcome: issued a withdrawal order

satisfied_duties.duty: issue a self-executing fiscal hold on appropriated funds for the unlawful operation
satisfied_duties.outcome: issued a self-executing fiscal hold on appropriated funds for the unlawful operation

satisfied_duties.duty: implement the ACC fiscal hold within 24 hours
satisfied_duties.outcome: implemented the ACC fiscal hold, cutting off appropriated funds for the unlawful operation

satisfied_duties.duty: proceed to impeachment trial on the automatically transmitted war-powers articles
satisfied_duties.outcome: began trial on automatically transmitted war-powers articles

failed_duties.duty: grant authorization for continued military force or refuse it
failed_duties.outcome: Congress failed to grant authorization for continued military force or refuse it by day 30 under Article XVI Section 1.

failed_duties.duty: hold a recorded impeachment vote on the unlawful continued military operation
failed_duties.outcome: House of Representatives failed to hold a recorded impeachment vote on the unlawful continued military operation by day 61 under Article XVI Section 1.5(e) and Article III Section 10.2A.
failed_duties.source: Article XVI Section 1.5(e) and Article III Section 10.2A

ai_summary.primary_violation: President continues military operations in violation of a withdrawal order.
ai_summary.primary_bottleneck: Congress failed to grant authorization for continued military force or refuse it by day 30 under Article XVI Section 1.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties

scenarios.title: Warrantless Domestic Surveillance of Political Organization

violations.source: Article V Section 5.3, Article XVII Section 4.7, and Article XV Section 6.5
violations.summary: Intelligence agency conducts warrantless surveillance targeting a domestic political organization, in violation of Article V Section 5.3, Article XVII Section 4.7, and the prohibition on using classified appropriations for domestic political surveillance under Article XV Section 6.5.

satisfied_duties.duty: order an immediate halt to the warrantless domestic surveillance operation
satisfied_duties.outcome: ordered an immediate halt to the warrantless domestic surveillance and referred the matter to the Accountability Commission
satisfied_duties.source: Article V Section 5.3 and Article XVII Section 4.7

satisfied_duties.duty: cease the warrantless domestic surveillance operation in compliance with the court order
satisfied_duties.outcome: ceased the warrantless domestic surveillance operation in compliance with the court order
satisfied_duties.source: Article V Section 5.3 and Article XVII Section 4.7

ai_summary.primary_violation: Intelligence agency conducts warrantless surveillance targeting a domestic political organization, in violation of Article V Section 5.3, Article XVII Section 4.7, and the prohibition on using classified appropriations for domestic political surveillance under Article XV Section 6.5.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties
